metadata.official_name: Iowa State University of Science and Technology

strategic_profile.executive_summary: Iowa State University is a massive public land-grant research university known for its excellence in engineering, agriculture, and applied sciences. Operating under the motto 'Science with Practice,' it offers highly accessible admissions while maintaining rigorous STEM-focused academic standards.
strategic_profile.market_position: Large Public Land-Grant R1 Research University

analyst_takeaways.insight: Admissions are highly deterministic, utilizing a mathematical formula (RAI) rather than holistic essay reviews.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants with strong GPAs and test scores are guaranteed admission, removing the unpredictability found at other institutions.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Unlike many large state schools, ISU does not broadly employ impacted or capped majors for incoming freshmen.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students can safely declare competitive majors like engineering or computer science without fear of immediate rejection based on major capacity.

analyst_takeaways.insight: High ROI, particularly for engineering and business graduates, with median 10-year earnings far exceeding national averages.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Offers a highly lucrative investment, especially for in-state students paying lower tuition.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A quintessential, spirited college town characterized by 'Iowa Nice' culture, dominated by Big 12 Cyclone athletics and a sprawling 800+ club network.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Centrally located in Ames with robust local transit and easy access to Des Moines; considered a safe and contained campus environment.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: An R1 institution spending over $420 million annually on R&D, offering undergraduates immediate access to agricultural robotics, biorenewables, and engineering labs.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for first-year applicants. Non-submitters are evaluated primarily on high school GPA and core course completion.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed due to rolling admissions structure.

gpa.notes: 59% of admitted students possess a GPA of 3.75 or higher.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking hands-on, highly ranked mechanical, aerospace, or agricultural engineering programs.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.notes: ISU's most popular major with exceptional graduate starting salaries.

colleges.name: College of Agriculture and Life Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Perfect for students interested in biosystems, agronomy, and animal science.

majors.name: Animal Science
majors.notes: Highly integrated with ISU's renowned College of Veterinary Medicine.

application_deadlines.notes: Applications open July 1st. Decisions are often returned within days.

primary_factors: Rigor of secondary school record (Core Courses)
holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Admission is formulaic, driven by the Regent Admission Index (RAI) rather than soft factors.

major_selection_tactics: Applying to a specific major does not restrict admission, as students are admitted to the university rather than competitive departments.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Not required, as primary admission grants access to the university as a whole.

financials.tuition_model: Flat rate with differentials for upper-division specific programs (like engineering/business).

scholarships.name: National Merit Full Tuition Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition for 8 semesters for Iowa residents named National Merit Scholars.

scholarships.name: Transfer Expedition Award
scholarships.benefits: Automatic award for non-resident transfer students from select states.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 3 or better generally accepted, depending on departmental review.
exceptions: Some engineering/science departments strictly require a 4 or 5.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Maximum 65 credits from 2-year colleges; final 32 credits must be completed in-residence.

what_it_takes: A solid GPA and core course load to meet the RAI threshold.
A passion for practical, hands-on learning (Science with Practice).
common_activities: Engineering and Agricultural clubs (e.g., Ames Flyers, Rodeo Club)
red_flags: Failing to complete required high school core courses (math, science, English).
insights: The 'Iowa Nice' culture is highly prevalent.
The university maintains an incredible 800+ student organizations.
Ames is routinely rated as a quintessential, supportive college town.
